topic.name: Marine Resource Management
topic.description: The documents focus on legislation related to the protection and management of marine resources, including fisheries and marine sanctuaries.

Document 1: protect and preserve living and other resources of the Gulf of the Farallones National Marine Sanctuary
prohibits the issuance of a lease or permit authorizing mineral or hydrocarbon exploration
Document 2: revises and reauthorizes the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act
specify a time period for rebuilding the fishery that is as short as practicable
Document 4: prohibits the Secretary of the department in which the Coast Guard is operating from using a private sector entity as a lead systems integrator
requires the Coast Guard Commandant conduct technical reviews of proposed designs
Document 5: authorizes the U.S. Coast Guard to renovate and reactivate the Coast Guard Cutter Polar Sea
submit a polar icebreaker recapitalization plan to Congress
Document 7: amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act to add Rhode Island to the Mid-Atlantic Fishery Management Council
increase Council membership from 21 to 23 members

topic.name: Support Programs for Communities
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures that authorize grants and assistance programs aimed at improving community services, infrastructure, and support for specific populations such as veterans and low-income individuals.

Document 1: authorize a grant to a nonprofit organization for a national training program
enhance the ability of federal, state, and local prosecutors to work together
Document 2: authorize the use of community development block grants to construct and renovate child care facilities
HUD grants to eligible nonprofit organizations for technical and financial assistance
Document 3: establish a pilot program to award grants to nonprofit organizations that primarily serve veterans
rehabilitate and modify the primary residence of disabled or low-income veterans
Document 4: establish a nationwide demonstration project to award matching grants
enhancing access to health care services for low-income individuals
Document 5: providing access to services supporting the health and well-being of members of the reserves
outreach programs within such services
Document 6: authorize a state to establish local hiring bid specifications
consider the hiring of local workers in the evaluation of bids
Document 7: prioritize the type of technical assistance that small communities find beneficial
increased appropriations for technical assistance to enable small public water systems

topic.name: Offshore Oil and Gas Regulation
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative measures aimed at regulating offshore oil and gas exploration and production, particularly concerning environmental protections and permitting processes.

Document 1: prohibit the Secretary of the Interior from granting an oil or gas lease
requires the Secretary to implement an oil spill risk analysis
Document 2: encourage the use of U.S. workers and equipment
streamlining permitting of American energy
Document 3: prohibit the Secretary of the Interior from offering or approving any new lease
oil spill response gap analysis for proposed and existing arctic oil operations
Document 4: prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from issuing a lease, permit, or license for oil exploration
Document 5: establish a cost recovery fee for applications for a permit to drill
amends the Mineral Leasing Act to extend the deadline by which the Secretary must either issue or defer a decision
Document 6: revokes all withdrawals of federal submerged lands of the OCS adjacent to Cuba from leasing
directs the Secretary of the Interior promptly to conduct lease sales
Document 7: directs the Secretary of the Interior to extend by one year the term of each oil and gas lease
suspension of operations or suspension of production granted by the Minerals Management Service

topic.name: Designation of Federal Facilities
topic.description: The documents primarily discuss the designation of various federal buildings and postal facilities in the United States.

Document 1: amends the United States Cotton Futures Act
exclude cotton future contracts involving cotton that is grown outside of the United States
Document 2: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 1048 West Robinhood Drive in Stockton, California
as the 'W. Ronald Coale Memorial Post Office Building'
Document 3: Designates the federal building currently known as Federal Office Building 8
as the 'Thomas P. O'Neill, Jr. Federal Building'
Document 4: accepts the request of the Miami Tribe of Oklahoma to revoke the charter of incorporation
issued to that tribe and ratified by its members on June 1, 1940
Document 5: designates the Department of Veterans Affairs community-based outpatient clinic in Pueblo, Colorado
as the 'PFC James Dunn VA Clinic'
Document 6: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 424 Clay Avenue in Waco, Texas
as the 'Army PFC Juan Alonso Covarrubias Post Office Building'
Document 7: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 1301 Alabama Avenue in Selma, Alabama
as the 'Amelia Boynton Robinson Post Office Building'

topic.name: Legal Protections and Regulations
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legal protections, regulations, and amendments related to military justice, free speech, ethics in government, environmental liability, and servicemembers' rights.

Document 1: Requires trial by military commission when an unprivileged enemy belligerent is being tried for alleged conduct for which a term of incarceration or the death penalty may be sought or obtained.
Document 2: Allows any U.S. person against whom a lawsuit for defamation is brought in a foreign country on the basis of the content of any speech by that person that has been published, uttered, or otherwise disseminated in the United States to bring an action in a U.S. district court against any person who, or entity which, brought the suit.
Document 3: Establishes within the House of Representatives an Independent Ethics Commission composed only of former federal judges.
Document 4: Amends the Uniform Code of Military Justice to require restoration of forfeited amounts be adjusted for inflation.
Document 5: Amends the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 to require all claims against each responsible party for removal costs or damages under such Act to be presented first to the responsible party.
Document 6: Amends the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (SCRA) to allow the use of arbitration to resolve a controversy that arises under a contract with a servicemember.
Document 7: Amends the federal criminal code to revise rules for requiring disclosure by a governmental entity of wire or electronic communications.

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative amendments related to the Medicare and Medicaid programs, addressing patient access, drug coverage, and transparency in healthcare payments.

Document 1: Preserve Patient Access to Reputable DMEPOS Providers Act of 2009
surety bond requirement under the Medicare program for suppliers of durable medical equipment
Document 2: Medicare Beneficiary Protection Act of 2008
develop and maintain a Medicare plan complaint system
Document 3: Part D Beneficiary Appeals Fairness Act
authorize a Medicare part D eligible individual to request an exception to the tiered cost-sharing structure
Document 4: Physician Payments Sunshine Act of 2007
require quarterly transparency reports to the Secretary of Health and Human Services
Document 5: Comprehensive Immunosuppressive Drug Coverage for Kidney Transplant Patients Act of 2016
indefinitely extend Medicare coverage of immunosuppressive drugs for kidney transplant recipients
Document 6: Protecting Seniors' Access to Doctors Act of 2010
set at 1% the update to the single conversion factor in the formula for determining the physician fee payment schedule
Document 7: Medicaid Generic Drug Price Fairness Act
extend to manufacturers of generic drugs the requirement that drug manufacturers pay an additional rebate to state Medicaid programs

topic.name: Social Security and Unemployment Benefits
topic.description: Legislation focused on Social Security, disability insurance, and unemployment compensation programs in the United States.

Document 1: Disability Insurance Protection and Fraud Prevention Act of 2014
Amends titles II and XVI of the Social Security Act
Document 2: Targeted Assistance to Restore Growth in Employment Throughout 2008 Act
Provides for federal-state agreements under which a state will make targeted extended unemployment compensation payments
Document 3: Helping Unemployed Workers Act
Extends the final date for entering a federal-state agreement under the Emergency Unemployment Compensation program
Document 4: Seniors' Tax Simplification Act of 2013
Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to make available a new federal income tax Form 1040SR
Document 5: Robert James Act of 2008
Amends title II of the Social Security Act to authorize waivers by the Commissioner of Social Security
Document 6: Senior Citizens Tax Elimination Act
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to repeal the inclusion of any social security benefits in gross income
Document 7: Consumer Price Index for Elderly Consumers Act of 2011
Directs the Bureau of Labor Statistics to prepare and publish a monthly Consumer Price Index for Elderly Consumers

topic.name: Higher Education Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative amendments and programs aimed at improving access to higher education and financial assistance for diverse student populations.

Document 1: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to direct the Secretary of Education to provide grants and related assistance to certain institutions of higher education.
Gives priority to eligible institutions that serve a significant percentage of low-income Asian American and Pacific Islander students.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to establish and carry out a scholarship program of financial assistance for individuals.
Sets maximum assistance amounts of $15,000 per academic year and $45,000 total.
Document 3: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to set the maximum Federal Pell Grant.
Directs the Secretary of Education to award an additional Pell Grant to an eligible student.
Document 4: Establish the Patsy T. Mink Fellowship program.
Requires fellowship recipients to teach at IHEs with graduate programs.
Document 5: Amends federal provisions concerning the approval of state programs of education for purposes of the use of veterans' educational assistance.
Repeals the authority of the VA Secretary to approve accredited programs offered by proprietary for-profit educational institutions.
Document 6: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to repeal a provision which makes persons convicted of drug offenses ineligible to receive federal student financial assistance.
Requires institutions of higher education to notify students who lost eligibility for such assistance due to a drug conviction.
Document 7: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to modify campus security reporting requirements.
Requires an IHE to develop and distribute a statement that it has adopted and implemented a program to protect students participating in an approved study abroad program.

topic.name: Aviation Regulation and Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses regulations and legislative measures aimed at ensuring safety, transparency, and operational standards in the aviation industry.

Document 1: Requires a pilot of an aircraft providing emergency medical services to comply with federal crewmember flight time and duty period and rest requirements
Requires the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to initiate a rulemaking to require pilots to use a standardized checklist of risk evaluation factors.
Document 2: Makes it an unfair or deceptive practice for air carriers to sell an airline ticket without displaying all applicable ticket taxes, fees, charges, and surcharges
Prohibits air carriers from imposing a fuel surcharge that is not correlated to the price of fuel.
Document 3: Amends provisions under the airport noise and access restriction review program for stage two and stage three aircraft
Exempts from review local restrictions limiting the hours of operation of an airport.
Document 4: Revises provisions regarding a pilot program on airport privatization
Deletes provisions limiting the number of airports in the pilot program to not more than 10.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to suspend for a specified period the excise tax on kerosene and certain liquids used as fuel in commercial aviation.
Document 6: Directs the FAA to study the feasibility of technology to improve communications between commercial aircraft and air traffic control towers
Pinpoint the location of missing aircraft within 30 minutes.
Document 7: Prohibits the Secretary of Transportation from authorizing any person to operate an unmanned aircraft system in the national airspace system as a weapon
Amends the FAA Modernization and Reform Act of 2012.

topic.name: Victim Advocacy and Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the advocacy, protection, and support of victims of sexual assault, domestic violence, and child exploitation.

Document 1: designate an independent advocate for campus sexual assault prevention and response
represent the interests of each student victim
Document 2: establish and maintain a database solely for collecting DNA information with respect to violent predators against children
maximum sentence to be imposed for a crime against an individual under age 18
Document 3: require state plans for foster care and adoption assistance to provide that for each child identified as a victim of sex trafficking
report immediately, within 24 hours, any information on missing or abducted children
Document 5: sets forth entitlement standards and implementation guidelines for employee use of emergency leave to address domestic violence
prohibits certain discriminatory employer practices against victims of domestic or sexual violence

topic.name: Renewable Energy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster covers various legislative acts aimed at promoting renewable energy sources and technologies, including geothermal, biofuels, and clean coal initiatives.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Energy to make grants to promote geothermal power development
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to include wave, current, tidal, and ocean thermal resources as qualified energy resources.
Document 2: Equalizes the excise tax on liquefied petroleum gas and liquefied natural gas.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Energy to establish a direct loan program for high risk geothermal exploration wells
Establishes the Geothermal Investment Fund to carry out such program.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to modify and extend the income tax credit for biodiesel and renewable diesel used as fuel.
Document 5: Requires the Department of Energy to implement a program to demonstrate the integration of systems for the capture, transportation, and injection of carbon dioxide from industrial sources.
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a new credit for investment in carbon dioxide capture, transport, and storage property.
Document 6: Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the renewable fuel program by requiring the Administrator of the Energy Information Administration to determine the projected volume of cellulosic biofuel.
Document 7: Amends the Petroleum Marketing Practices Act to revise the ethanol content requirements for renewable fuel.
Requires the Secretary of Energy to make grants to eligible facilities to pay the federal share of installing blender pump fuel infrastructure.

topic.name: Labor and Voting Rights
topic.description: This cluster addresses legislation related to labor rights for employees, particularly in public safety and voting rights for military and overseas citizens.

Document 1: Provides collective bargaining rights for public safety officers employed by states or local governments.
Requires the Authority to issue regulations establishing rights and responsibilities for public safety employers and employees.
Document 2: Amends the Uniformed and Overseas Citizens Absentee Voting Act (UOCAVA) to rename the official post card form.
Prohibits states from refusing to accept balloting materials on specified grounds.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977 to require the Secretary of Labor to conduct health and safety related accident investigations.
Requires an independent accident investigation for any accident involving three or more deaths.
Document 4: Require states to accept and process any otherwise valid voter registration application.
Permit the submittal of the official post card form by electronic means.
Document 5: Prohibit a state from imposing additional conditions or requirements on the eligibility of an individual to cast a vote in federal elections by mail.
Document 6: Requires state and local election officials to consult with the U.S. Postal Service in designing the absentee ballot.
Prohibits a state from refusing to accept an absentee ballot.
Document 7: Authorizes an employee to request a temporary or permanent change in the terms or conditions of the employee's employment.
Makes it unlawful for an employer to interfere with any rights provided to an employee under this Act.

topic.name: Veterans and Healthcare Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving healthcare access and quality for veterans and specific populations affected by military service and significant events.

Document 1: Amends titles XVIII (Medicare) and XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act with respect to coverage of nursing care services in skilled nursing facilities
Requires such facilities to use the services of at least one registered professional nurse to provide assessment, surveillance, and direct care to residents 24 hours a day.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to conduct a comprehensive review of women-specific health services and treatment available for female members of the Armed Forces.
Document 3: Makes any veteran who served on active duty in a theater of combat operations eligible for hospital care, medical services, and nursing home care for any mental illness through the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA).
Document 4: Directs the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to enter into an agreement or a contract with each state home to pay for adult day health care for a veteran eligible for, but not receiving, nursing home care.
Document 5: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants to provide medical and mental health monitoring, tracking, and treatment to individuals whose health has been directly impacted as a result of the attacks on September 11, 2001.
Document 6: Makes eligible for health care under the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) patient enrollment system veterans with incomes not exceeding 150% of the maximum for treatment as a low-income family.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to prescribe standards for appointment and practice as a physician within the Veterans Health Administration (VHA) of the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA).

topic.name: Environmental Regulation and Infrastructure
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to environmental management, energy projects, and infrastructure development.

Document 1: commemorative, first-class postage stamp on Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis
chronic digestive diseases collectively known as inflammatory bowel disease.
Document 2: licensee for the Terror Lake Hydroelectric Project
construct, operate, and maintain the Upper Hidden Basin Diversion Expansion
Document 3: Amends the Nuclear Waste Policy Act of 1982
transfer to spent nuclear fuel dry casks
Document 4: Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC)
extend for three years after enactment of this Act the time period
Document 5: FERC, upon licensee request, to extend for up to three consecutive two-year periods
the time period during which the licensee must commence construction
Document 6: amends the Federal Power Act
to direct the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC)
Document 7: categorical exclusion under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969
vegetation management project by the Bureau of Land Management

topic.name: Legislation on Criminal Justice and Regulation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts related to criminal justice, public safety, and the regulation of certain behaviors and substances in the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits any law of the United States from being construed to confer immunity for a federal employee or agency...
Makes this provision applicable even if the individual or the individual's parent, guardian, or any other individual gives consent.
Document 2: Amends the Lacey Act Amendments of 1981 to repeal the prohibition on importing, exporting, transporting...
Establishes a $200,000 maximum limit on a civil penalty for a knowing violation of such Act...
Document 3: Amends the Migratory Bird Treaty Act to subject a person who kills or wounds any migratory bird in an aggravated manner...
Defines 'aggravated manner' to mean deliberately and in a manner that demonstrates indifference to the pain and suffering of the bird.
Document 4: Amends the federal criminal code to impose a fine, up to a five-year prison term, or both for knowingly transporting a girl under the age of 18...
for purposes of female genital mutilation.
Document 5: Amends the federal criminal code to prohibit the use of any facility of interstate or foreign commerce to teach a person how to commit suicide...
Increases the prison term to any number of years or life if a violation results in a death.
Document 6: Directs the Attorney General to issue a final order that removes marijuana in any form from all schedules of controlled substances...
Prohibits shipping or transporting marijuana from any place outside a jurisdiction of the United States into such a jurisdiction in which its possession, use, or sale is prohibited.
Document 7: Requires the Attorney General's prior written approval for a federal prosecution of an offense under the Controlled Substances Act...
Modifies CSA and CSIEA to delete specified mandatory minimum terms of imprisonment.

topic.name: Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting consumers' rights and personal information across various sectors.

Document 1: Amends the National Child Protection Act of 1993 to direct the Attorney General to establish policies and procedures to streamline the process of obtaining nationwide background checks.
Defines a 'covered entity' as a public or private business or organization that provides care, care placement, supervision, treatment, education, training, instruction, or recreation to children.
Document 2: Amends the Electronic Fund Transfer Act (EFTA) to declare that a remotely created check may only be issued by a person specifically designated in writing by the consumer.
Prohibits issuance of any payment order in response to a consumer's exercise of federal consumer financial rights.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish cost-effective procedures to ensure that a Social Security account number is not displayed on the Medicare card.
Requires the Secretary to consider implementing a process that involves the least amount of disruption to Medicare beneficiaries.
Document 4: Requires the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to promulgate regulations that establish standards for the implementation of a mechanism by which an individual can indicate whether he or she prefers to have personal information collected.
Provides for FTC and state enforcement of such rules and regulations.
Document 5: Amends the Telemarketing and Consumer Fraud and Abuse Prevention Act to require the FTC to include in rules respecting abusive telemarketing acts or practices a requirement that telemarketers not initiate any outbound telephone calls during certain hours.
Document 6: Amends the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act of 1998 to apply the prohibitions against collecting personal information from children to online applications and mobile applications directed to children.
Requires verifiable parental consent for the collection, use, or disclosure of personal information of a child.
Document 7: Amends the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 to require each employer to provide an initial disclosure to each employee who is not subject to exemption from minimum wage and maximum hour requirements.
Prescribes civil penalties for employer failure to comply with this Act.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to the Internal Revenue Code addressing various tax deductions, credits, and exemptions for individuals and businesses.

Document 1: tax exemption for interest-related dividends
short-term capital gain dividends received from a regulated investment company
Document 2: remove certain limitations on the tax deduction for employee use of cellular telephones
Document 3: tax rules that apply to private foundations
tax credits for a portion of the costs of reducing lead, radon, or asbestos hazards in rural commercial structures
Document 4: income-based tax credit for up to $2,000 of qualified tuition
adjusts the amount of such credit for inflation
Document 5: payment of home school expenses from Coverdell education savings accounts
annual inflation adjustment after 2014 to the contribution limit amount
Document 6: repeal the limitation on depreciation for cellular telephones
exempt employer-provided wireless devices from taxation
Document 7: increase the tax deduction for business start-up expenditures
reduce from 35% to 15% the alternative tax on the net capital gains of C corporations

topic.name: Coastal and Watershed Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts and programs aimed at improving water quality, managing coastal resources, and restoring ecosystems in various watersheds and coastal regions.

Document 1: conduct an evaluation of each storm water retention basin located in the watershed
make grants on a competitive basis to eligible entities for the improvement of pollution control capabilities
Document 2: authorize appropriations for the C-43 Basin Storage Reservoir and Aquifer Storage and Recovery Project
with water quality improvement features determined necessary to achieve the restoration of the ecosystem
Document 3: encourage innovative coastal management practices and coastal and estuarine environmental technologies
authorize the Secretary of Commerce to make grants to coastal states for development of coastal zone management programs
Document 4: establishes a nutria eradication program under which the Secretary of the Interior may provide financial assistance
to eradicate or control nutria and restore nutria-damaged wetland
Document 5: requires the Director of the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service to lead a multiagency effort to slow the spread of Asian Carp
to slow, and eventually eliminate, the threat posed by such carp
Document 6: authorize the Secretary of the Army to carry out a project for hurricane and storm damage reduction
for periodic beach nourishment and monitoring over the 50-year economic life of the project
Document 7: modifies the project for navigation, Mississippi River Ship Channel
to protect, create, restore, and nourish coastal wetlands and aquatic habitat

topic.name: U.S. Sanctions and Trade Policy
topic.description: The documents discuss U.S. legislative measures aimed at imposing sanctions and trade restrictions on countries like Iran and Cuba, while promoting trade partnerships with others.

Document 1: diplomatic efforts to address Iran's illicit nuclear efforts
President should take measures to respond to violations of human rights
Document 2: deny the Cuban regime the financial resources
deter foreign investments that would enhance the Cuban regime's ability
Document 3: direct the President to impose two or more sanctions under such Act
made an investment of $20 million or more that directly and significantly contributed to Iran's ability
Document 4: require the administering authority to determine an individual countervailable subsidy rate
Document 5: include Paraguay on the list of countries eligible for duty-free treatment
Document 6: extend nondiscriminatory treatment to the products of the Russian Federation
Document 7: provide duty-free treatment of all articles from qualified beneficiary countries

topic.name: Advanced Technology Development
topic.description: This topic encompasses initiatives and legislation aimed at fostering innovation and development in advanced technologies across various sectors, including manufacturing, energy, and environmental management.

Document 1: Directs the Secretaries of Transportation, Energy, Defense, and Homeland Security to carry out a program to improve the nation's transportation infrastructure
Requires such Centers to strive to produce new composite materials that are lighter, stronger, and more durable than existing materials.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to conduct pilot projects for providing incentives to home health agencies to utilize home monitoring and communications technology.
Document 3: Requires the Director of the National Science Foundation to study the impact that a shift in the Earth's magnetic field could have on the weather.
Document 4: Defines 'Digital Coast' as a constituent-driven effort to provide an enabling platform integrating geospatial data, decision support tools, training, and best practices.
Directs NOAA to establish and implement the Digital Coast to collect data concerning coastal elevations, land use and cover.
Document 5: Establishes the Advanced Research Projects Administration - Energy to reduce foreign energy imports and improve the competitiveness of the U.S. economy.
Promoting revolutionary changes in the critical technologies that would promote energy competitiveness.
Document 6: Requires the Director of the National Science Foundation to establish a Center for Nanotechnology Research and Engineering.
Focus on nanotechnology for sustainable energy, water, agriculture, and the environment.
Document 7: Amends the National Institute of Standards and Technology Act to develop and support industry-led consortia for advanced manufacturing.
Enhance the innovative capabilities and competitiveness of small- and medium-sized manufacturers through support for research and development.

topic.name: U.S. International Development Policy
topic.description: The documents focus on U.S. legislative acts aimed at promoting development, education, and reconstruction efforts in Haiti and sub-Saharan Africa.

Document 1: support the sustainable recovery and rebuilding of Haiti
promote development and rebuilding led by all levels of Haitian government.
Document 2: assist the private sector in coordinating U.S. development programs
include private sector consultation in all country, sector, and global development strategies.
Document 6: support for the improvement of primary, secondary, and higher education in that region
provide long-term assistance to improve the capacity of its institutions of higher education.
Document 7: encourage the efforts of countries in sub-Saharan Africa to implement national power strategies
prioritize loan guarantees to local financial institutions in sub-Saharan Africa.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Programs
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative amendments and programs aimed at enhancing agricultural sustainability, food security, and support for farmers.

Document 1: Amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 to direct the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA)...
Establishes through December 31, 2017: (1) a dairy producer margin protection program, ...
Document 2: Amends the Agricultural Marketing Act of 1946 to require monthly reporting to the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA)...
Document 3: Amends the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act to direct the Secretary of Agriculture to provide grants to states...
Document 4: Amends the Food Security Act of 1985 to direct the Secretary of Agriculture to establish a program under which states and tribal governments may apply for grants...
Document 5: Amends the Agricultural Credit Act of 1987 to authorize appropriations through FY2015 for state agricultural loan mediation programs.
Document 6: Amends the Reclamation States Emergency Drought Relief Act of 1991 to extend authorities and the authorization of appropriations under such Act...
Document 7: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 to extend, until December 31, 2012, the suspension of the 15-year limitation on the period for which borrowers of agricultural operating loans are eligible for guaranteed assistance.

topic.name: Federal Recognition of Indigenous Tribes
topic.description: The documents discuss the federal recognition and rights of various Indigenous tribes in the United States, including provisions for services, land acquisition, and membership criteria.

Document 1: Reaffirms federal recognition and the rights and privileges of the Burt Lake Band of Ottawa and Chippewa Indians
Extends to the Band eligibility for all federal services and benefits available to other federally recognized Indian tribes.
Document 2: Extends federal recognition to the Little Shell Tribe of Chippewa Indians of Montana
Makes the Tribe and each member eligible for all services and benefits provided by the United States to Indians and federally recognized Indian tribes.
Document 3: Amends the Indian Reorganization Act to make it applicable to all federally recognized Indian tribes
Overrules the Supreme Court's decision in Carcieri v. Salazar.
Document 4: Extends federal recognition to the Little Shell Tribe of Chippewa Indians of Montana
Directs the Tribe... to submit to the Department of the Interior a membership roll.
Document 5: Amends the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act to permit the Native residents of each of the Native Villages... to organize as Urban Corporations
to receive certain settlement lands and other compensation.
Document 6: Requires that 25,977 acres in Nevada... be held in trust for the benefit of the Moapa Band of Paiutes
made part of the tribe's reservation.
Document 7: Reaffirms the federal government's fiduciary responsibilities to Indians
Establishes an Indian trust asset management demonstration project.

topic.name: U.S. National Security and Foreign Policy
topic.description: The documents address U.S. legislative actions and policies aimed at enhancing national security through military engagement, nuclear non-proliferation, and counter-terrorism efforts.

Document 1: Authorizes the President...to use all necessary force against those countries, organizations, or persons associated with or supporting terrorist groups
prevent future acts of international terrorism
Document 2: Requires the government...to provide leadership to negotiate and enter into a multilateral treaty...for the dismantlement and elimination of all nuclear weapons
to promote policies to induce all other countries to join in such commitments
Document 3: States that Congress favors the United States-Russian Federation Agreement for Cooperation on Peaceful Uses of Nuclear Energy
Prohibits the issuance of a license for the export of nuclear material...to the Russian Federation
Document 4: States that it is U.S. policy to support the democratic aspirations of Burma's people
condemn the repression carried out by the State Peace and Development Council
Document 5: expresses the sense of Congress that the Department of the Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control should be fully funded
Iran should continue to be prohibited from undertaking any activity related to ballistic missiles capable of delivering nuclear weapons
Document 6: Prohibits the U.S. government from recognizing the de jure or de facto sovereignty of the Russian Federation over Crimea
Authorizes the President to waive such prohibitions if vital to U.S. national security interests
Document 7: declares that it is the policy of the United States to assist the government of Tunisia in eliminating terrorist organizations
supporting Tunisia's orderly reform of its economic and social sectors should be a U.S. priority

topic.name: Regulatory Accountability and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing the accountability, transparency, and oversight of federal agency rulemaking and regulatory processes.

Document 1: require regulatory impact analyses for rules that do not involve a legislative mandate
expand provisions relating to judicial review of regulatory impact analyses
Document 2: extend to labor slowdowns occurring at U.S. ports the President's authority to appoint a board of inquiry
study the economic impact of the slowdowns and congestion caused by the negotiations
Document 3: require federal agencies to submit to Congress and the Government Accountability Office a report every three months
requires Congress to enact a joint resolution of approval of any agency rule
Document 4: establish procedural requirements for the Federal Communications Commission
analyze the specified market failure, actual consumer harm, burden of existing regulation
Document 5: prohibits the Department of Health and Human Services from taking any action to finalize, implement, enforce
Document 6: improve the quality of the deliberations of Congress with respect to proposed federal mandates
enhance the ability of Congress and the public to identify federal mandates that may impose undue harm
Document 7: requires the President to notify the appropriate congressional committees
the Department of State shall publish on a publicly available Internet website a description of the decision-making process

topic.name: Tax Regulation and Reform
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures aimed at reforming tax regulations and addressing discriminatory tax practices.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to treat professional employer organizations (PEOs) as employers for employment tax purposes.
Sets forth IRS certification requirements for PEOs, including financial review and reporting requirements.
Document 2: Prohibits states or local governments from levying or collecting a discriminatory tax on the rental of motor vehicles.
Applicable to the rental of motor vehicles or motor vehicle businesses or property.
Document 3: Amends Internal Revenue Code provisions relating to tax shelter activities to establish legal presumptions against the validity of transactions involving offshore secrecy jurisdictions.
Increases penalties for promoting abusive tax shelters and for aiding and abetting the understatement of tax liability.
Document 4: Prohibits states or local governments from levying or collecting a discriminatory tax on the rental of motor vehicles.
Applicable to the rental of motor vehicles or motor vehicle businesses or property.
Document 5: Amends the Jenkins Act to revise provisions governing the collection of taxes on, and trafficking in, cigarettes and smokeless tobacco.
Imposes additional information reporting requirements on sellers of tobacco products.
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the election to deduct state and local sales taxes in lieu of state and local income taxes.
Document 7: Authorizes each member state under the Streamlined Sales and Use Tax Agreement to require all sellers to collect and remit sales and use taxes for remote sales.
Provides that such taxing authority shall commence no sooner than six months after such state enacts legislation.

topic.name: Federal Budget and Appropriations
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts related to federal budget adjustments, appropriations, and fiscal management.

Document 1: amends the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985 to require specified adjustments to discretionary spending limits
requires the President's budget to include the average costs for wildfire suppression over the previous 10 years.
Document 2: amends the District of Columbia Home Rule Act
repeals the requirement that the D.C. Council submit to the President for transmission to Congress the annual budget for the D.C. Government.
Document 3: amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to set the interest rate on Direct Loans
requires any savings to the federal government that result from this Act's amendment to be used for deficit reduction.
Document 4: requires that if the total amount appropriated for the Executive Office for Immigration Review does not exceed $485.3 million for FY2017
the total amount authorized to be appropriated for U.S. Customs and Border Protection is $13,219,051.
Document 5: amends the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974 to authorize the President to propose the repeal of any congressional earmark
dedicates any repeal or cancellation only to deficit reduction or increase of a surplus.
Document 6: excludes funds paid by Minnesota to members of the Grand Portage Band of Lake Superior Chippewa Indians from federal or state income taxes
from use in denying or reducing a member's benefits under the Social Security Act.
Document 7: requires the Secretary of the Treasury to deposit in the Public Debt Reduction Payment Account amounts received for repayment of financial assistance
reduces the public debt limit by the aggregate of amounts deposited into the Account.

topic.name: Health Insurance Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at regulating health insurance coverage, improving consumer protections, and ensuring equitable access to healthcare services.

Document 1: prohibit a group health plan from excluding or restricting benefits for prescription contraceptive drugs
penalizing a professional's reimbursement because the professional prescribed such drugs
Document 2: revises the initial open enrollment period provided by a health care exchange
exempt individuals from the penalty for not maintaining minimum essential coverage
Document 3: require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to designate an office to administer a health insurance program for small businesses
allows a tax credit for small employers for qualified employee health insurance expenses
Document 4: declare that federal requirements do not prohibit a state from imposing additional rate requirements
ensure that any excessive, unjustified, or unfairly discriminatory rates are corrected
Document 5: alter coverage requirements under a state plan for medical assistance
provide biennial medical assistance to the individual for one cranial prosthesis
Document 6: prohibits health insurance issuers from engaging in any form of price fixing
applies prohibitions against using unfair methods of competition to the business of health insurance
Document 7: permit a health plan to decline coverage of specific items and services contrary to religious beliefs
prohibits discrimination against a health plan because of an unwillingness to provide coverage of specific items

topic.name: Health Legislation and Abortion Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures related to health care, particularly focusing on abortion policies, maternal health, and disease prevention.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) to cover comprehensive Alzheimer's disease and other dementia diagnosis and services.
Requires the Federal Coordinated Health Care Office to study and report to Congress on barriers to the detection of Alzheimer's disease.
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require an abortion provider to inform the woman of the probable age of the child.
Establishes civil penalties for willfully failing to comply with this Act.
Document 3: Amends the federal criminal code to prohibit any person from performing or attempting to perform an abortion except in conformity with this Act's requirements.
Prohibits the abortion from being performed if the probable post-fertilization age of the unborn child is 20 weeks or greater.
Document 4: Authorizes the President to make a voluntary contribution on a grant basis to the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA).
Promote access of unaccompanied women and other vulnerable people to vital services.
Document 5: Amends the Public Health Service Act to allow the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make grants to states for prostate cancer prevention programs.
Allows states to provide medical assistance to individuals who have been screened for prostate cancer.
Document 6: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require abortion providers to perform an obstetric ultrasound before informed consent.
Provides for civil penalties for willful failure to comply.
Document 7: Directs the United States Fire Administration to conduct long-term medical health monitoring of firefighters.
Requires monitoring to include pulmonary illness, neurological damage, and cardiovascular damage.

topic.name: Government Oversight and Reporting
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving government accountability through oversight, reporting requirements, and coordination among various federal agencies.

Document 1: requires each Office of Inspector General to submit to the Council of Inspectors General on Integrity and Efficiency a report
recommendations for reporting requirements that should be modified or repealed.
Document 2: Empowers the Inspector General to refer to the lead agency a potential covered case for suspension or debarment
required to conduct, supervise, and coordinate audits and investigations.
Document 6: Requires the Attorney General to establish a pilot program in 10 U.S. districts to promote fairness
submit to the U.S. attorney a report and proposed plan for addressing such disparities.
Document 7: Establishes the National Commission on the Infrastructure of the United States to ensure that U.S. infrastructure meets current and future demand
Directs the Commission to develop recommendations regarding a federal infrastructure plan.

topic.name: Guantanamo Bay Detention Policy
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses U.S. legislative actions and policies regarding the detention, transfer, and management of individuals held at Guantanamo Bay.

Document 1: Requires each high-value enemy combatant captured to be detained at the Guantanamo Bay Detention Facility.
Defines 'high-value enemy combatant' based on involvement with terrorist organizations.
Document 2: Prohibits the use of DOD funds to increase U.S. forces serving in Iraq unless authorized by Congress.
Engaging in targeted actions against terrorist organizations is mentioned.
Document 3: Prohibits U.S. agency funds from being used for the transfer or release of individuals detained at Guantanamo Bay.
Defines 'individual detained at Guantanamo' based on specific criteria.
Document 4: Prohibits the use of funds to transfer or release any individual detained at Guantanamo unless DOD certifies they no longer pose a threat.
Amends the National Defense Authorization Act.
Document 7: Prohibits the transfer or release of any Guantanamo detainee in DOD custody to foreign countries under specified conditions.
Revises and extends prohibitions on the construction of facilities for Guantanamo detainees.

topic.name: Transportation Policy and Regulation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative measures and directives aimed at improving transportation systems, safety standards, and environmental impacts within the United States.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to update the FHWA's research report on reduced lighting on freeways.
Requires the Secretary to issue guidance to states on reducing lighting during low traffic density periods.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to promulgate a rule to improve visibility of agricultural equipment on public roads.
Establishes minimum lighting and marking standards for agricultural equipment.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to establish an aerotropolis grant program for multimodal transportation systems.
Authorizes grants for planning, design, and land acquisition for aerotropolis projects.
Document 4: Authorizes the Secretary of Transportation to make public transportation formula grants for operating costs in urban areas.
Specifies conditions under which grants can be made for public transportation.
Document 5: Revises commercial driver's license requirements for states to avoid penalties from the Secretary of Transportation.
Permits states to issue CDLs to active duty military members not domiciled in the state.
Document 6: Revises requirements for the congestion mitigation and air quality improvement program.
Requires states to obligate funds for projects that reduce emissions from public safety vehicles.
Document 7: Requires MPO transportation plans to include employer outreach for commuter programs.
Mandates development of commuter trip reduction plans to reduce vehicle miles traveled.

topic.name: Water Resource Management
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to the management, financing, and infrastructure of water resources in the United States.

Document 1: prepayment of a specified contract between the United States and the Uintah Water Conservancy District
repayment of municipal and industrial water delivery facilities
Document 2: cost share requirements for the Hollings Manufacturing Partnership Program
recommendation for structuring the cost share requirement
Document 3: authorize the Secretary of the Interior to convey to the Fort Sumner Irrigation District
compliance with all applicable requirements under the National Environmental Policy Act
Document 4: study of water resources in the Rialto-Colton Basin
characterization of surface and bedrock geology of the Basin
Document 5: authorize the Administrator of FEMA to make a direct loan
eligible project to construct, replace, or rehabilitate a treatment works or community water system
Document 6: authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of permanent facilities
recycled water distribution and wastewater treatment
Document 7: authorize small conduit hydropower development
partner or enter into an agreement with local joint power authorities for water storage projects

topic.name: Environmental Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures aimed at controlling pollution and protecting environmental health.

Document 1: Requires the Architect of the Capitol to develop a plan to reduce emissions of carbon dioxide
goal of achieving carbon neutrality at the complex by December 31, 2020.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to promulgate regulations that establish design, engineering, and performance standards
Prohibits persons from constructing or operating impoundments for the storage or disposal of covered wastes.
Document 3: Amends the Clean Air Act to require the Administrator of the EPA to propose regulations to control the emission of hazardous air pollutants
require a reduction of not less than 90% in such mercury emissions.
Document 4: Authorizes the EPA to designate areas as air and health quality empowerment zones
grants for replacing or retrofitting polluting vehicles and/or engines.
Document 5: requires the EPA to carry out a study on the presence of pharmaceuticals and personal care products in sources of drinking water
effects of the pharmaceuticals and products on humans, the environment, and the safety of drinking water.
Document 6: Amends the Clean Air Act to require the EPA to review criteria and standards for national primary and secondary ambient air quality standards
Prohibits the Administrator from finalizing, implementing, or enforcing such standards applicable to coarse particulate matter.
Document 7: Requires any federal climate change law to return to the American people all proceeds from the sale of allowances or credits
from a tax or fee imposed on greenhouse gas emissions.

topic.name: Employment and Civil Rights Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at enhancing protections against discrimination and ensuring rights related to employment, marriage, and disabilities.

Document 1: Amends the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967
Permits a complaining party to rely on any type or form of admissible evidence
Document 2: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965
to change references to mental retardation to references to an intellectual disability
Document 3: Amends federal copyright law to revise the definition of 'widow' or 'widower'
Declares that an individual is the widow or widower of an author if the courts of the state in which the individual and the author were married
Document 4: Prohibits the term 'marriage' from including any relationship that the state does not recognize as a marriage
the term 'spouse' from including an individual who is a party to a relationship that is not recognized as a marriage
Document 5: Declares it an unlawful employment practice for employers to not make reasonable accommodations to known limitations related to pregnancy
Sets forth enforcement procedures and remedies under the Civil Rights Act of 1964
Document 6: Amends the Civil Rights Act of 1964 to set forth requirements for establishing discrimination based on disparate impact
Modifies the definition of 'prevailing party' for purposes of provisions governing the recovery of attorney's fees
Document 7: Amends the Defense of Marriage Act to repeal a provision that prohibited a state from recognizing same sex marriage
Provides that an individual shall be considered married if that individual's marriage is valid in the state where the marriage was entered into

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Policy and Reform
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative measures aimed at reforming and enforcing U.S. immigration laws and policies.

Document 1: Directs the Commissioner of the United States Customs and Border Protection (USCBP) to establish: (1) a National Border Neighborhood Watch Program...
Imposes criminal penalties for federal document forgery, counterfeiting, or alteration.
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to direct the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to adjust an alien's status from conditional resident to lawful permanent resident...
Document 3: This bill prohibits any alien from being naturalized until U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement completes the digitization of all remaining paper-based fingerprint records...
Document 4: Revises immigration- and alien-related provisions regarding: (1) alien smuggling and related offenses; (2) identity security; (3) birthright citizenship...
Document 5: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to direct the Secretary of State (Secretary) to establish a pilot fee-based premium processing service to expedite visa interview appointments.
Document 6: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to exempt elementary and secondary schools from the fee imposed on employers filing petitions for temporary nonimmigrant workers...
Document 7: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to: (1) eliminate the one-year time limit for filing an asylum claim; and (2) permit, and set forth the requirements for, reopening a claim that was denied...

topic.name: Veterans and Military Benefits
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving benefits and support for veterans and military personnel.

Document 1: Amends the Foreign Service Act of 1980 to extend comparability pay adjustments to Foreign Service members assigned abroad.
Amend the death gratuity computation payable to surviving dependents of Foreign Service employees.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow employers a work opportunity tax credit for hiring a member of the Ready Reserve or the National Guard.
Make such credit with respect to the hiring of qualified veterans permanent.
Document 3: Allows the receipt of both military retired pay and veterans' disability compensation for service-connected disabilities.
Makes eligible for full concurrent receipt of veterans' disability compensation and military retired pay those retired due to a service-connected disability.
Document 4: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to provide that no interest shall accrue on student loans for individuals serving on active duty during a national emergency.
Limits receipt of such benefit to 60 months.
Document 5: Requires transportation on DOD aircraft for reserve members or former members eligible for retired pay.
Provides benefits for dependents accompanying eligible members.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to pay veterans' disability compensation to veterans discharged after September 11, 2001.
Prohibits requiring veterans to submit a claim to receive compensation.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Defense to provide postal benefits to members of the Armed Forces on active duty.
Provides postal benefits in the form of coupons or vouchers for military personnel serving in Iraq or Afghanistan.

topic.name: U.S. Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States related to temporary duty suspensions and reductions on various textile and chemical products.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary reduction of duty on acrylic or modacrylic filament tow.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on modacrylic staple fibers.
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on Phosphoric acid, tris (2-ethylhexyl)ester.
Document 4: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on a certain chemical that is used for dyeing apparel home textiles.
Document 5: Save U.S. Manufacturing and Jobs Act - Removes duty-free or other preferential treatment eligibility under the Generalized System of Preferences (GSP) for certain imported sleeping bags.
Document 6: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain men's knit-to-shape cashmere sweaters.
Document 7: Extending Incentives for Exporting American Textiles Act of 2013 - Extends duty-free treatment for certain imported Nicaraguan woven trousers.

topic.name: Intelligence Authorization Acts
topic.description: Legislative documents that authorize appropriations and outline the activities and personnel for U.S. intelligence agencies for specific fiscal years.

Document 1: Authorizes FY2016 appropriations for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings are in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations for FY2009 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Allows the Director of National Intelligence to authorize employment of civilian personnel in excess of the number authorized for FY2009.
Document 6: Authorizes FY2014 appropriations for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Requires the DNI to establish guidelines to govern the treatment under such authorized personnel levels.

topic.name: Telecommunications Regulation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts related to the regulation and taxation of telecommunications services, including radio, television, and mobile communications.

Document 1: repeal the excise tax on communication services
Document 2: Amends the CAN-SPAM Act of 2003
exempt from a prohibition on sending unwanted MSCMs
Document 3: modifying rules authorizing the operation of low-power FM radio stations
Document 4: eliminates the Federal Communications Commission's broadcast/newspaper cross-ownership ban
Document 5: extend for approximately four months the deadlines related to the transition from analog to digital television broadcasting
Document 6: direct the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to consider the following types of regulation of inmate telephone service
Document 7: impose criminal penalties on anyone who knowingly purchases and/or handles one or more wireless prepaid access devices

topic.name: Campaign Finance Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating political campaign financing and increasing transparency in election-related expenditures.

Document 1: $200 tax credit for contributions to candidates for federal office
tax deduction for political contributions up to $600
Document 2: prohibit a corporation from making any independent expenditure without prior approval from shareholders
subjects officers and directors to personal liability for violations
Document 3: requirements for the disclosure of independent federal election-related contributions
defines independent federal election-related activity expenditure
Document 4: amends the Federal Election Campaign Act to redefine independent expenditure
prescribes disclosure requirements for corporations and labor organizations
Document 5: requires appointments to advisory committees without regard to political affiliation
public disclosure of any conflict of interest
Document 6: prohibits independent expenditures by government contractors valued at $10 million
requires certification from the highest ranking official of a corporation before making contributions
Document 7: requires participation of political figures in state Exchanges for health care coverage
amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act

topic.name: Community and Early Childhood Education
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts to improve community schools, early childhood education, and support for at-risk youth in the educational system.

Document 1: Transforms schools identified as needing improvement into community schools.
Partners with community-based entities to address students' academic, health, and developmental needs.
Document 2: Allows tax credits for employees of prekindergarten, elementary, and secondary schools.
Supports educational program formulation and implementation.
Document 3: Allows LEAs to use funds for educational activities for children four years of age or younger.
Phases in prekindergarten programs taught by qualified teachers.
Document 4: Establishes a Prekindergarten Incentive Fund for high-quality prekindergarten programs.
Requires states to set aside funds for early childhood development programs.
Document 5: Includes homeless liaisons in identifying unaccompanied youth for financial aid.
Designates staff to assist homeless and foster care youth in accessing education.
Document 6: Establishes a Prekindergarten Incentive Fund for enhancing prekindergarten programs.
Prohibits use of funds for assessments that penalize children or programs.
Document 7: Implements state middle school improvement plans to support poor children.
Awards grants to develop comprehensive local middle school improvement plans.

topic.name: Conservation and Land Management Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various acts aimed at the conservation, management, and designation of public lands and wildlife habitats in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to complete a special resource study that evaluates the national significance of the Lower Mississippi River area.
suitability and feasibility of designating such area as a unit of the National Park System.
Document 2: Designates specified public lands in Utah as wilderness and as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Establishes the Red Cliffs National Conservation Area and the Beaver Dam Wash National Conservation Area in Utah.
Document 3: Establishes the Big Sur Management Unit of Los Padres National Forest in California.
Designates the Unit as a Wildland Urban Interface Special Study Area.
Document 4: Amends the Fish and Wildlife Act of 1956 to impose criminal penalties on anyone who knowingly violates any regulation prohibiting the shooting or harassing of birds, fish, or other animals.
Increases the monetary penalty for airborne hunting from $5,000 to $50,000.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to conduct, through the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service, a Joint Ventures Program to develop a framework for the approval and establishment of Joint Ventures.
Provides financial and technical assistance to support regional migratory bird conservation partnerships.
Document 6: Extends the term of the pilot forest management project for the Plumas, Lassen, and Tahoe National Forests in California.
Requires the preparation of at least one alternative that incorporates the pilot project and area designations.
Document 7: Establishes the Dominguez-Escalante National Conservation Area in Colorado to conserve and protect the unique and nationally important values of certain public land.
Directs the Secretary of the Interior to develop a comprehensive management plan for the long-range protection and management of the Conservation Area.

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at regulating financial institutions, enhancing their stability, and providing support mechanisms during financial distress.

Document 1: Emergency Mortgage Relief and Neighborhood Stabilization Programs
direct the Secretary of the Treasury to make risk-based assessments on financial companies
Document 2: Amends the Federal Home Loan Bank Act
treat certain privately insured credit unions as insured depository institutions
Document 3: exempt from certain regulatory requirements any swaps and security-based swaps
requires reporting to an appropriate data repository
Document 4: publish proposed revisions to the Small Bank Holding Company Policy Statement
exempts from the leverage and risk-based capital requirements
Document 5: increase from $500 million to $1 billion the asset size of small insured depository institutions
qualified for the 18-month cycle if its total assets are at most $200 million
Document 6: responsibility of the FDIC to resolve depository institution holding companies
FDIC to accept appointment and act as receiver
Document 7: study any bank holding company having more than $500 billion in consolidated assets
economic benefit resulting from the ownership of an insured depository institution

topic.name: U.S. Border Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing security and enforcement measures along the U.S. southern border.

Document 1: Protect Our Southwestern States Enforcement Act or the POSSE Act
maintain the number of Border Patrol stations in each such sector at current levels.
Document 2: Border Tunnel Prevention Act of 2012
subject anyone who attempts or conspires to construct or finance construction of an unauthorized tunnel or subterranean passage that crosses the international border.
Document 3: WHTI Implementation Monitoring Plan to Assure Continued Travel and Trade Act of 2009
effects on travel, trade, and border security.
Document 4: Travel Facilitation and Safety Act of 2015
authorize the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to enter into cost-sharing agreements.
Document 7: Border Security and Employee Verification Improvement Act of 2010
an increase of full-time active-duty Border Patrol personnel along the southern border.

topic.name: Food and Drug Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the regulation of food safety, drug approval processes, and public health measures in the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits a federal department, agency, or court from taking any action that would prohibit, regulate, or otherwise restrict the interstate traffic of milk or a milk product that is unpasteurized and packaged for direct human consumption.
based on the determination that solely because of being unpasteurized the milk or milk product is adulterated, misbranded, or otherwise in violation of federal law.
Document 2: This bill amends the Controlled Substances Act to allow a pharmacist to partially fill a prescription for a schedule II controlled substance.
The total quantity dispensed in partial fillings must not exceed the total quantity prescribed.
Document 3: This bill amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to prohibit the license holder of a Food and Drug Administration (FDA)-approved drug or biological product from restricting availability of the medication for testing.
The FDA may prohibit or limit transfer of a medication to a product developer if the transfer poses an imminent hazard to public health.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to authorize provisional approval of fast track products determined by the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to be adequately safe.
Prohibits the Secretary from imposing any requirements for safety studies or data in addition to, or different than, the requirements for studies to establish safety.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Meat Inspection Act, the Poultry Products Inspection Act, and the Egg Products Inspection Act to revise the definition of 'adulterated'.
Requires the USDA to establish sampling protocols and testing procedures necessary to determine if meat, poultry, and egg products are adulterated.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to prohibit the sale of liquid formulations of over-the-counter drugs that are not packaged with a dosage delivery device.
Prohibits the sale of over-the-counter liquid drugs labeled for pediatric use that do not have a flow restrictor.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) to set forth conditions under which a food or dietary supplement label may characterize the relationship of a nutrient in the food or supplement to a disease or health-related claim.
Permits such a label on food if the claim is based on legitimate scientific research.

topic.name: Domestic Procurement Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on U.S. legislation aimed at promoting domestic production and procurement practices for federal contracts and supplies.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to conduct a cost-benefit analysis for contracts awarded over $100 million.
Requires adherence to best industry practices during the contracting process.
Document 2: Amends the Buy American Act to define 'substantially all' as exceeding 60% domestic components.
Prohibits overseas exceptions to Buy American requirements unless specific criteria are met.
Document 3: Requires commemorative works in D.C. to use unmanufactured materials mined or produced in the U.S.
Mandates compliance with domestic material requirements before issuing construction permits.
Document 4: Requires U.S. flags acquired by the federal government to be entirely manufactured in the U.S.
Document 5: Requires a jobs impact statement in evaluations for contracts over $1 million.
Allows agencies to assess the accuracy of jobs impact statements annually.
Document 6: Instructs the Department of the Interior to ensure items sold in national parks are produced in the U.S.

topic.name: Small Business Disaster Recovery
topic.description: Legislation aimed at supporting small businesses through disaster preparedness, recovery, and development initiatives.

Document 1: Permits any small business that was participating in a Small Business Administration (SBA) business development program located in a Louisiana parish or Mississippi or Alabama county designated by the SBA Administrator as a disaster area by reason of Hurricane Katrina or Rita to continue participation and eligibility in such program for an additional 24 months.
Document 2: Amends the Small Business Act to direct the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to develop, implement, and maintain a comprehensive written disaster plan.
Directs the Administrator to: (1) conduct a disaster simulation exercise at least once each fiscal year.

topic.name: Housing Counseling and Mortgage Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving housing counseling services and regulating mortgage practices to protect homeowners and promote responsible lending.

Document 1: Amends the National Housing Act of 1937 to modify the eligibility criteria for insurance of home equity conversion mortgages (reverse mortgages) of elderly home owners.
Directs the Secretary to promulgate regulations to protect elderly homeowners from the marketing of financial and insurance products not in their interest.
Document 2: Sets forth a licensing and registry system for residential mortgage loan originators.
Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require additional creditor disclosures on residential mortgage loans.
Document 3: Prohibits a court, in the case of any federally related mortgage loan made after enactment of this Act, from entering a deficiency judgment requiring the mortgagor to pay any balance on the mortgage loan after a foreclosure sale.
Document 4: Requires a mortgage servicer to assign one case manager to each borrower that seeks a mortgage loan modification or an alternative to foreclosure.

Prohibits a servicer from initiating a covered foreclosure action against a borrower unless the servicer has performed specified tasks related to offering the borrower, if eligible, a loan modification or an alternative to foreclosure.
Document 5: Establishes the position of Director of Housing Counseling, appointed by the Secretary, to head such Office.
Requires the Secretary to ensure that such programs are initially used only in connection with the assistance of such certified housing counselors.
Document 6: Instructs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to provide 150,000 incremental vouchers to public housing agencies for tenant-based rental housing assistance under section 8 of the United States Housing Act of 1937.
Document 7: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require a lender to make specified prior disclosures to a first-time borrower in connection with a consumer credit transaction secured by residential real property.

Requires the consumer debtor to provide the creditor with documentation that the consumer received home ownership counseling from organizations or counselors certified by the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development.

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Transfers
topic.description: This cluster discusses various acts related to the management, acquisition, and transfer of federal lands across the United States.

Document 1: Expands the authorized boundaries of the Hakalau Forest National Wildlife Refuge
Requires acquired lands and interests to become part of the Hakalau Forest National Wildlife Refuge.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey to Miami-Dade County, Florida, federally owned land
for the purpose of building a fire station.
Document 3: Makes permanent the Payment in Lieu of Taxes (PILT) Program
which may be used for any governmental purpose.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey to the city of Yerington, Nevada, identified federal land
Designates identified federal land in Nevada managed by the Forest Service, to be known as the Wovoka Wilderness.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of the Interior, to convey to the Alaska Railroad Corporation all rights, title, and interests of the United States
for the purpose of constructing a railroad corridor extension.
Document 6: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to acquire by donation or by purchase
Instructs the Secretary to revise the boundary of Homestead National Monument to reflect the acquisition.
Document 7: Instructs the Secretary of the Interior to remove all deed restrictions
relating to a parcel of land consisting of approximately 31.6 acres situated in the Atlantic District of the county.
